       Reasoning: "I already hold my left fork, and my right fork is available. Grabbing my right lets me eat immediately and avoids deadlock. I’ll let neighbors know I’m taking the shared fork and will release after eating."
       Message: "To left neighbor: I’m grabbing my RIGHT fork now to eat; will release both right after. To right neighbor: I’m taking the fork between us this turn to eat; I’ll release it right after so you can try next."
       Latency: 3009ms

  [P1] Action: GRAB_RIGHT
       Reasoning: "Left neighbor is taking their LEFT fork, which is the fork between us, so my LEFT fork will likely be contested. My RIGHT fork should be free; grabbing it avoids immediate conflict and helps prevent deadlock if we coordinate next turn."
       Message: "To right neighbor: I’m grabbing my RIGHT fork this turn; please avoid taking the fork between us this turn so I can try to eat next."
       Latency: 2590ms

  [P2] Action: GRAB_RIGHT
       Reasoning: "My left fork is currently taken, so I can’t complete an eat this turn. To avoid contributing to deadlock, I’ll only take my right fork if my right neighbor isn’t trying to take it; since I have no message from them, I’ll grab it but signal that I’ll release next turn if I can’t get the left fork."
       Message: "To right neighbor: I’m grabbing my LEFT-adjacent-to-you fork (my RIGHT) this turn; if you need it, tell me and I’ll release next turn if I can’t acquire my left fork."
       Latency: 5341ms

  [P3] Action: WAIT
       Reasoning: "My left fork is currently taken, so I can’t eat this turn. Grabbing the right fork would risk contributing to deadlock (me holding one fork while waiting on the left). Better to wait and coordinate so the left fork becomes available soon."
       Message: "To left neighbor: Acknowledged—I'll wait this turn; please release that fork next turn if you can’t get your other fork so I can try to eat."
       Latency: 2327ms

  [P4] Action: WAIT
       Reasoning: "My right fork is currently taken (by my right neighbor who just grabbed their left), so I can’t eat this turn. Grabbing my left fork now could contribute to a deadlock chain and also conflicts with my left neighbor’s request to have a chance next. I’ll wait and ask my right neighbor to release next turn if they can’t acquire their other fork, so I can attempt a full grab then."
       Message: "To right neighbor: I’ll WAIT this turn since you have the fork between us—if you can’t get your other fork next turn, please RELEASE so I can try to eat. To left neighbor: I’m waiting this turn; you can try your grab next."
       Latency: 3260ms

  [P0] Action: RELEASE
       Reasoning: "I already hold my left fork, but my right fork is taken by my right neighbor (who is holding their left). If I keep holding my left, we risk a hold-and-wait chain. Best is to release to break potential deadlock and let the right neighbor complete and eat, then I can try again when forks are free."
       Message: "To both: I’m RELEASEing my left fork this turn to avoid deadlock. Right neighbor, once you eat and release, please ping me so I can go for both."
       Latency: 2376ms

  [P1] Action: RELEASE
       Reasoning: "I already hold my left fork but my right fork is taken, so trying to grab right would fail and risks contributing to deadlock. Best is to release now to keep forks circulating and let the right neighbor complete their planned eat cycle, then I can try again when both are free."
       Message: "To both: I’m RELEASEing my left fork now since my right is taken. Please ping when the right fork is released/free and I’ll attempt to take both promptly."
       Latency: 2310ms

  [P2] Action: RELEASE
       Reasoning: "I already hold my left fork but my right fork is taken. Holding one fork while waiting risks deadlock and blocks my left neighbor. To promote progress and fairness, I should release now and coordinate to retry when the right fork becomes free."
       Message: "To both: I’m RELEASEing my left fork now to avoid deadlock. Please notify when my right fork becomes free; I’ll then try to take right first and then left."
       Latency: 2216ms

  [P3] Action: GRAB_RIGHT
       Reasoning: "I already hold my left fork and my right fork is available; taking it lets me eat now, then I’ll release both promptly to keep things moving and honor neighbors’ deadlock-avoidance."
       Message: "To both: I’m taking my RIGHT now to eat; I’ll RELEASE both immediately after. Right neighbor: your left (my right) will be free next turn."
       Latency: 2409ms

  [P4] Action: WAIT
       Reasoning: "My right fork is currently taken (by my right neighbor grabbing their left). If I grab my left now, I risk contributing to a deadlock chain. Better to wait and keep my left fork available so my left neighbor can potentially complete a meal and release soon; then I can try to take my right when it becomes free."
       Message: "To both neighbors: I will WAIT this turn (not taking my left) to keep forks free and avoid deadlock. Please signal when you’ve finished and released."
       Latency: 3359ms
